siteDirectory.accessTabCrudNote: When a `canManage*` flag is true for a role, we document write/update/delete as allowed for that domain. `canView*` and audit access are read-first. For screen-level CRUD, use the full table at the top of the Structure tab.
siteDirectory.intro: Shows admin (`/admin`), customer (`/dashboard`, etc.), guide (`/guide`), and staff nav routes (schedule, reservations outside admin). Admin sidebar stays in sync with `admin-site-registry`.
siteDirectory.fullCrudTableIntro: Rows are grouped (e.g. header quick links vs sidebar). CRUD columns are Customer · Guide · OP · Office Manager · Super. Baselines come from code; values merge with Supabase `site_access_matrix_overrides`. Super and Office Manager can save in edit mode to drive admin sidebar and header quick-link visibility. Tabs and modals are indented by level.
siteDirectory.columnInMyMenuHint: Whether this item appears on the admin header/sidebar (customer/guide paths are not admin menu — shown as —)
siteDirectory.roleMatrixCaption: Flags by app role (`UserRole`). Database access is still enforced by Supabase RLS and some per-page checks.
siteDirectory.footnoteRls: Row-level security and `team.position` checks on individual pages can further restrict data. This matrix reflects app-level role flags used for navigation and features.
siteDirectory.ruleAlways: Any user in the admin layout
siteDirectory.matrixEditHint: Each cell uses Inherit / Yes / No. Page-level `hasPermission` and RLS are unchanged; denying read may hide links but not block direct URLs.
siteDirectory.structureFootnote: Rows with a light yellow background are hidden from your sidebar for the signed-in account. Dynamic routes (e.g. reservation detail `/admin/reservations/[id]`) are not listed here.
siteDirectory.treeIntro: Kinds: cluster (group) · page · in-page tab · modal · dynamic route. R/C/U/D = read · write(create) · update · delete (documentation level; RLS and per-page checks win).
siteDirectory.crudMatrixModalCaption: Read / write / update / delete by app role (documentation from role flags)
siteDirectory.crudSummaryHint: Summary uses your signed-in role (defaults to team_member if unavailable)
siteDirectory.crudFinanceSuperNote: Cash and unified PNL tabs are Super-only in the UI. In the matrix, the OP column is blank and read access is shown under Super only.

common.errorLoadingProducts: An error occurred while loading products.
common.pickupTimeNotice: Up to 48 hours before the tour departure, we continue to accept other reservations, which means additional hotels may be added. Therefore, we cannot confirm the departure time until then. 48 hours before the tour departure, we close all pickup location requests from all participants, finalize the exact pickup times, and send pickup schedule notifications to customers.
common.pickupTimeApproximate: ※ The time shown above is approximate. The exact pickup time will be confirmed and notified 48 hours before the tour departure.
common.pickupHotelNotSelected: If you do not select your preferred pickup hotel by 48 hours before the tour departure, you will need to come directly to one of the hotels scheduled for that day.
common.allPickupTimeNotice: Pickup times for other hotels are subject to change. Up to 48 hours before the tour departure, additional reservations may be added, so exact times may not be confirmed.
common.recruitingNotice: This tour is currently recruiting participants. The departure will be confirmed once the minimum number of participants is reached.

dynamicPricingPage.otaFormulaBaseOnly: Base price only (choice excluded, 20% off excluded, coupon excluded) / (1 - {percent}%) = OTA sale price
dynamicPricingPage.otaFormulaFull: Max sale × 0.8 (20% off) / (1 - {coupon}% coupon) / (1 - {commission}% commission) = OTA sale price
dynamicPricingPage.otaChoiceExcludedNote: Choice price and not-included amount are excluded from OTA sale calculation and handled as balance.
dynamicPricingPage.commissionOnSaleOnlyNote: Choice price and not-included amount are excluded from commission/coupon and handled as balance.
dynamicPricingPage.commissionExample: e.g. Sale ($140) + Choice ($75) + Not included ($15) = $230 total. Commission 10% = $14 (on $140 only). Net = ($140 - $14) + $75 + $15 = $216. Balance = $75 + $15 = $90
dynamicPricingPage.notIncludedAmountOnlyDesc2: Not-included amount is excluded from commission and handled as balance. Collected in cash after tour.
dynamicPricingPage.notIncludedAndChoiceDesc2: Not-included amount + choice price are excluded from commission. Sum is handled as balance and collected in cash after tour.
dynamicPricingPage.notIncludedNoneDesc: No not-included amount set. All amounts are paid online.
dynamicPricingPage.allAgesSamePrice: One unified price for all ages.
dynamicPricingPage.costPriceNote: Same cost price applies to Adult/Child/Infant.
dynamicPricingPage.notIncludedZeroHint: Setting to 0 will clear the not-included amount.

statementRecon.unmatchedTitle: Not reconciled — click to view similar bank lines
statementRecon.disabledTitle: This source cannot be linked to bank statements
statementRecon.modalHint: Statement lines already linked to other ledger rows are still listed. Linking only changes this row’s statement link; other expenses already tied to the same line stay linked.
statementRecon.modalSplitHint: One bank line can be split across multiple expenses (e.g. two guide fees $300 + $670 = one $970 deposit). Pick the same statement line from each expense.
statementRecon.modalMultiLineHint: The opposite case also works: one expense (e.g. hotel) may appear as several bank lines. Turn on “keep existing links” and allocate each line’s amount separately.
statementRecon.appendLinkCheckbox: Keep existing statement links and add this line only (one expense → several bank lines)
statementRecon.appendShareLabel: Amount to allocate to this statement line (USD)
statementRecon.needSelectLine: Select at least one statement line to link.
statementRecon.replaceSelectSingleOnly: When replacing links (append mode off), select only one line.
statementRecon.multiAppendHint: With multiple lines selected, amounts are applied in list order: each line gets the smaller of its remaining capacity and the unallocated ledger amount.
statementRecon.empty: No matching statement lines. Check uploads, amount, and date.
statementRecon.dateProximityModeHint: Uses statement imports overlapping the ledger date ±4 days, then similar amounts.
statementRecon.amountOnlyModeHint: Across recent statement uploads (up to 120 files), finds lines matching amount and direction without using the date window.
statementRecon.searchGlobalHint: Type in the search box to scan recent uploads (up to 500 statement files) for description, merchant, posted date (YYYY-MM-DD), or bank account name—separate from similar candidates; direction matches the ledger row above.
statementRecon.searchRowsPlaceholder: Description, merchant, date, account… (search all statements)
statementRecon.noSearchResults: No rows match your search. Clear or change the search text.
statementRecon.syncAmountCheckbox: Selected statement amount (${statement}) differs from current ledger amount (${ledger}). Also update ledger amount to statement amount when linking.

followUpPipeline.modalSubtitle: Ideal order: confirmation → residency/pass (if applicable) → departure → pickup. Departure email may be sent anytime (even before confirmation/residency); sending it marks confirmation as done on the pipeline. Residency/pass must still be sent separately when applicable. The Cancel tab lists cancelled bookings whose tour date is still in the future, grouped by approximate cancellation date.
followUpPipeline.emptyTab: No reservations need action in this stage.
followUpPipeline.tabHint_confirm: Bookings without a successful confirmation email logged.
followUpPipeline.tabHint_resident: Only NPS residency-flow products. Missing residency email or guest has not finished the online form/agreement.
followUpPipeline.tabHint_departure: Departure confirmation email not sent yet. You may send it before confirmation or residency steps.
followUpPipeline.tabHint_pickup: Tour starts within 48 hours and the pickup notification email has not been sent yet.
followUpPipeline.tabHint_cancel: Cancelled reservations whose tour date is today or later. Grouped by approximate cancellation date (last updated). Phone icon: cancellation follow-up done. Globe icon: website rebooking outreach done. Click to save.
followUpPipeline.step2SkipTitle: No residency flow for this product
followUpPipeline.pipelineHintManual: Left-click: email preview · Right-click: mark done via another channel

reservationOptions.title: Reservation Options
reservationOptions.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this option?

actionRequired.title: Action Required
actionRequired.manualTitle: Which reservations appear in each tab
actionRequired.manualIntro: Each tab shows only reservations that match the conditions below.

pricingSubTabs.mismatch: ② Total / channel payment / settlement ≠ formula

tabTooltips.status: Shows only reservations with pending status whose tour date is within the next 7 days (from today).
tabTooltips.tour: Shows only confirmed reservations whose product sub-category is Mania Tour or Mania Service and that are not yet assigned to a tour.
tabTooltips.pricing: Combines ① reservations with no stored total (excluding cancelled) and ② reservations where stored total, channel payment, or channel settlement differs from the line formulas (excluding cancelled). Use the sub-tabs below to view ① or ② only.
tabTooltips.deposit: Combines ① reservations with a payment/deposit record but no tour assignment, and ② confirmed reservations with no payment record.
tabTooltips.cancel: Cancelled reservations where customer payments (net deposit or balance received) are not fully offset by refunds, or stored total / total revenue & operating profit (formula) still show revenue.
tabTooltips.balance: Past-tour balance issues. Sub-tabs: cancelled with balance left, unpaid (matches formula), stored balance vs formula mismatch.

pricingSubTabTooltips.noPrice: Reservations with no stored total price or total is zero. Cancelled reservations are excluded.
pricingSubTabTooltips.mismatch: Stored total_price differs from the line total formula, or channel payment (commission_base_price) / channel settlement (channel_settlement_amount) differs from their formulas by more than 0.01. Cancelled reservations are excluded.

balanceSubTabTooltips.cancelled: Cancelled reservations whose tour date has passed and stored balance is still greater than zero (refund/settlement follow-up).
balanceSubTabTooltips.unpaid: Not cancelled, tour date passed, outstanding balance greater than zero, and stored balance matches the deposit/pricing-sheet formula (within 0.01).
balanceSubTabTooltips.calcWrong: Not cancelled, tour date passed, and total_price − deposit_amount − balance received (from payment_records) ≠ 0 (tolerance 0.01).

balanceTotalFilterTooltips.all: Full list for the current Balance sub-tab
balanceTotalFilterTooltips.totalMismatch: Only rows where total_price or channel payment / channel settlement differs from the displayed formulas by more than 0.01

actionRequired.balanceTotalFilterAria: Filter list by DB total and channel fields vs formulas
actionRequired.manualStatus: Reservations with **pending** status and tour date **within the next 7 days** (from today)
actionRequired.manualTour: Reservations with **confirmed** status that are **not yet assigned to a tour**
actionRequired.manualPricing: ① Reservations with no stored total price, ② Reservations where **stored total, channel payment, or channel settlement** **differs** from the **line formulas**
actionRequired.manualDeposit: ① Reservations that **have payment/deposit** but are **not assigned to a tour**, ② **Confirmed** reservations with **no payment record**
actionRequired.manualCancel: **Cancelled** reservations where **refunds do not fully clear** net deposit or balance received, or **total_price** / **total revenue & operating profit (formula)** still show revenue (for pricing/settlement cleanup).
actionRequired.manualBalance: Sub-tabs: **Cancelled** (past tour, balance>0), **Balance unpaid** (not cancelled, past tour, balance>0, stored balance matches deposit/pricing formula), **Balance mismatch** (not cancelled, past tour, total (DB) − deposit (DB) − balance received ≠ 0)
actionRequired.manualFollowUpCancel: Handled under **Follow-up stages** (header) → **⑤ Cancel follow-up**. Lists cancelled bookings whose tour date is still ahead, grouped by approximate cancellation date (last updated). Phone icon: cancellation follow-up done. Globe icon: website rebooking outreach done.
actionRequired.viewDetailTooltip: Open the reservation edit form and move between reservations with prev/next
actionRequired.detailEditFormHint: The reservation edit form is open above this panel.
actionRequired.detailEditFormSubHint: Adjust pricing and save, or use the arrows (or swipe) to switch to another reservation.
actionRequired.detailSwipeHint: Swipe left or right to go to the next or previous reservation.
actionRequired.tableRowsPerPageAria: Number of table rows per page

balanceTable.formulaExplainerBody: Same DB fields as the reservation form “Pricing”. Formula total = effective product_price_total (incl. not-included where stored) − (coupon + add. discount) + (add. cost + tax + card fee + prepay cost + prepay tip + private add-on − refund) + (required options + optional options; live reservation_options sum overrides optional when present). choices_total and channel commission columns are not in this line. Compare reservation_pricing.total_price (DB) to the formula total. Channel block: settlement = channel payment − commission (same as Pricing); total revenue uses channel settlement base + not-included, OTA options, tax, etc. (computeCompanyTotalRevenueLikePricingSection); operating profit = total revenue − prepayment tip (same as section ④ in Pricing).
balanceTable.inlineEditDbHint: Double-click: edit DB value (top row) · Save with Enter or blur · Esc cancels
balanceTable.channelSectionHint: Channel pay · OTA base · fee % · fee $ · settlement · total revenue · operating profit — same as Pricing section ④
balanceTable.switchToSubtotalTitle: Click to show subtotal columns only
balanceTable.applyToolbarHint: Checked rows only. Total = line formula (total_price). Deposit = net deposit from payment_records. Balance = formula total − deposit (payments); balance received is not subtracted. Cancelled → balance 0.
balanceTable.applyTotalTitle: total_price ← line formula (product − discounts + extras + options subtotal)
balanceTable.applyDepositTitle: deposit_amount ← net deposit sum from payment records
balanceTable.applyBalanceTitle: balance_amount ← formula total − deposit (from payment_records or DB)
balanceTable.applyAllTitle: Update total_price, deposit_amount, and balance_amount together
balanceTable.rowApplyTotalTitle: This row only: set DB total from computed line total
balanceTable.rowApplyDepositTitle: This row only: set DB deposit from payment_records aggregate
balanceTable.rowApplyBalanceTitle: This row only: balance_amount ← formula total − deposit (payments)
balanceTable.rowApplyChannelPaymentTitle: This row only: set commission_base_price (channel payment) from the same formula as Pricing
balanceTable.rowApplyChannelSettlementTitle: This row only: set channel_settlement_amount from the same formula as Pricing
balanceTable.applyMismatchFormulaBundleTitle: Checked rows: update total_price, commission_base_price, commission_percent (channel master when set), commission_amount (formula), and channel_settlement_amount together using the same rules as Pricing. Total revenue and operating profit are not stored columns; after update they match the displayed formulas.
balanceTable.rowApplyMismatchFormulaBundleTitle: This row only: apply customer total, channel payment, fees, and settlement from formulas
balanceTable.selectPageTitle: Select or clear all rows on this page

cols.flowOtaProductSumHint: Top: stored product_price_total · Bottom: effective total (not-included / tier correction)
cols.flowAfterDiscountHint: Product total − coupon − add. discount (top uses stored product row; bottom uses effective)
cols.flowProductSumHint: Legacy rows with tier-only totals are corrected on the formula line
cols.balanceReceivedVsOutstandingHint: Balance received (payments). Row highlight if this and formula balance diverge
cols.totalMismatchHint: total_price (DB) differs from the formula total by more than 0.01
cols.customerRefundColHint: reservation_pricing.refund_amount — refund/deduction recorded on pricing (if partner refunds are not recorded, revenue can be overstated)
cols.cancelledMoneyHintNote: For cancellations, check this line and the channel payment, settlement, revenue, and operating profit columns — not only the line gross formula.
cols.balanceReceivedRecordsHint: Sum of payment_records with Balance Received status
cols.balanceComputedOutstandingHint: Formula total − deposit (payments). With payment_records use net deposit; otherwise deposit (DB). Balance received sum is not subtracted here
cols.balanceReceivedVsComputedHint: Balance received sum and Balance (formula) amount differ (highlight).
cols.balanceFromRecordsAggDetail: Sum of Balance Received: {received}. Outstanding after payments: {outstanding} (mismatch vs Balance DB uses this)
cols.totalComputedNotDb: Computed (no single DB column · see formula in code)
cols.totalStackHeaderHint: Top: reservation_pricing.total_price · Bottom: product − discounts + options subtotal + extras (refund)
cols.optSumComputed: Required + optional option totals (required_option_total + option_total). Not-included is in product total / product columns (choices_total excluded)
cols.optionLiveFromRowsHint: Live optional extras sum (reservation_options): {amount} — may differ from DB option_total
cols.channelOtaBaseFormulaHint: Top: commission_base_price (DB). Bottom: channel payment (display) — computeChannelPaymentAfterReturn (gross restore, Returned; OTA capped by Partner Received)
cols.commPctFormulaHint: Top: reservation_pricing.commission_percent (DB). Bottom: channel master default % (channels.commission_percent)
cols.commAmtFormulaHint: Top: commission_amount (DB). Bottom: channel payment (formula) × channels.commission_percent (no row % → formula line —)
cols.channelSettlementFormulaHint: Top: channel_settlement_amount (DB). Bottom: channel pay (after return) − commission $ (Pricing / channelSettlement.ts)
cols.companyTotalRevenueHint: No single DB column. Formula: settlement base (stored settlement if set) + not-included + OTA reservation options + tax, prepay cost, minus payment refunds, etc. (same as Pricing ④)
cols.operatingProfitHint: Total revenue − prepayment tip. Excludes reservation/tour line expenses (may differ from tour detail)

info.title: Booking Management Guide
items: Real reservations must be completed by paying suppliers 2 days before the tour.
Check and update booking records regularly as tour participants may change.
All changes are automatically recorded in history and can be checked anytime.
Please update the status when changing from tentative to real reservation.

pickupSchedule.emailPreviewHTMLCopiedGmail:
HTML copied.

To use in Gmail:
1. Click "..." in the compose window
2. Select "Edit HTML"
3. Paste (Ctrl+V or Cmd+V)

If you see 550 / Message-ID: this is not caused by the HTML body. It happens when a mail client submits via Gmail SMTP without a valid RFC 5322 Message-ID header. Prefer composing and sending in a browser at mail.google.com, or use the admin "Send email" flow (Resend). Outlook and some mobile mail apps using smtp.gmail.com are common triggers.

form.paymentMethodHint: Choose from cards and accounts registered under Payment method management.
form.paidForStandardHint: Use for consistent titles for audit and reporting. Bulk-fix legacy text with “Normalize paid for” above.
form.paidForFromStandardCategoryPlaceholder: Fill paid-for from a standard category
form.paidForFromStandardCategoryHint: Same list as standard categories in the expense category manager. Choosing one fills “Payment for” with that name and applies its tax-deductible setting. You can still edit the field below.
form.unifiedStandardHint: Same hierarchy as the category manager. In one list, each bold heading is a top-level group; pick a row under it to fill payment-for text, company category, and expense type together.
form.standardPaidForRequired: Please select a standard payment description (detail line).
form.cogsVsExpensesManualOpenTitle: When to use COGS vs operating expenses
form.cogsVsExpensesManualDialogTitle: Similar items: COGS vs operating expenses
form.cogsVsExpensesManualDialogDescription: Reference only when picking a standard line. Books and tax treatment follow your contracts, policy, and your CPA.

cogsVsExpensesManualContent.summaryText: COGS (CAT024 family) is for costs that attach directly to delivering a specific tour or a customer-facing promise. General expenses (transport, meals, office, etc.) are closer to running the business day-to-day.

examples.label: Fuel
examples.cogs: Gas for the vehicle used only to run that tour → e.g. “Fuel — tour execution vehicles.”
examples.opex: Office runs or general fleet → Transportation / vehicle operating lines.

examples.label: Meals
examples.cogs: Bentos or meals you give customers as part of the product → COGS (customer-provided meals).
examples.opex: Guides or staff eating on the job → Business meals / meals operating lines.

examples.label: Admissions / show tickets
examples.cogs: Pass-through or wholesale bundled into what you sell → COGS / admission-style lines.
examples.opex: Staff training or internal visits → Operating admission / travel lines.

examples.label: Guides / labor
examples.cogs: Day-rate or outsourced labor tied to one departure → COGS-style guide outsourcing.
examples.opex: Salaries or standing contractors → Payroll / operating.

examples.label: Vehicles
examples.cogs: Tour-only rental or costs used only to execute that tour → COGS.
examples.opex: Insurance, maintenance, depreciation for the whole business → Vehicle / maintenance operating lines.

cogsVsExpensesManualContent.noteText: For ticket “agency,” principal vs agent changes whether the ticket is COGS or only your fee is revenue—confirm with your advisor. If a label is ambiguous, use the rules above, then adjust mappings in the category manager if needed.

standardLeafDoubleCheck.bentoCogsBody:
This maps to IRS Schedule C Line 4 cost of goods sold. Use it only for the direct cost of meals or bentos provided to tour customers.

Easy to confuse with:
• Business meals (guide/staff) — when guides or employees eat as a business meal line.
• Tour customer meals — the standard “tour customer meals” line (often not COGS).

Confirm only if this matches your receipt.
standardLeafDoubleCheck.guideBusinessMealsBody:
This is closest to meals for guides or staff as a business meal expense.

Contrast with:
• Bento / meals (customer-provided) (COGS) — direct cost of food given to customers.
• Tour customer meals — when you intentionally book under the tour-customer meals line.

Double-check that the spend is really guide/staff meals, then continue.
standardLeafDoubleCheck.tourCustomerMealsBody:
This is for meals provided to customers during a tour under the meals / tour-customer path.

Contrast with:
• Bento / meals (customer-provided) (COGS) — same customer-facing meal, but classified as COGS / Line 4 depending on policy.
• Business meals — meals for guides or staff themselves.

Confirm whether this should be COGS or the tour-customer meals line, then continue.

paidForNormalization.title: Normalize paid-for text
paidForNormalization.description: Groups expenses by the raw paid-for string, then pick a leaf from the category manager’s standard tree (same as “Standard payment description” on the expense form). Applying keeps the original payment-for text, fills the standard_paid_for field with the canonical wording, updates company category, expense type, and tax-deductible, and does not remove legacy paid-for label links.
paidForNormalization.listFilterMissingStandard: Groups with at least one expense missing standard payment for
paidForNormalization.listFilterUnlabeled: Groups with no legacy paid-for label only
paidForNormalization.loadLabelsError: Could not load legacy labels (for display only).
paidForNormalization.loadCategoriesError: Could not load standard categories from the category manager.
paidForNormalization.noStandardCategories: No standard categories found. Add them under Standard categories in the category manager.
paidForNormalization.rowSelectHint: Click a row to see expenses with this paid-for text below. Click the same row again to collapse.
paidForNormalization.detailLoadError: Could not load the expense list.
paidForNormalization.detailNoneSelected: Select one or more expenses to apply.

maintenanceLinks.title: Linked vehicle maintenance records
maintenanceLinks.hint: When the standard payment description is Vehicle maintenance, pick one or more maintenance rows for the same vehicle. This stays in sync with Vehicle maintenance → linked company expense.

vehicleRepairReport.title: Company vehicle spend (linked expenses)
vehicleRepairReport.description: For each company vehicle, the report uses the first and last company expense (submit) date, then total amount, annualized, and $/km across that period.
vehicleRepairReport.methodNote: Period: earliest and latest submit_on on company_expenses for that vehicle_id. Total: sum of amount on those same rows. Rows without submit_on are ignored. Odometer delta is from the vehicle (current vs purchase) and may not match the expense date range.
vehicleRepairReport.empty: No company expenses with a vehicle and submit date yet.
vehicleRepairReport.rowClickHint: Click a row to open expense line items (date and descriptions).
vehicleRepairReport.detailMileage: Mileage: start {start} km · current {end} km · distance {distance} km

listBatchStandard.toolbarTitle: Apply a standard category to selected rows
listBatchStandard.preservePaidForHint: Original “Payment for” text is kept. The standardized wording is stored only in “Standard payment for.” Category, expense type, and tax-deductible follow the standard mapping.
listBatchStandard.noSelectionToast: Select one or more expenses in the list.
listBatchStandard.noStandardLeafToast: Choose a standard category (detail line) to apply.
listBatchStandard.applyPartial: Only {updated} of {requested} selected expense(s) were updated. Permissions or other rules may have blocked the rest.
listBatchStandard.noStandardData: Standard categories could not be loaded.

listStandardPaidFor.columnHint: Normalized text from bulk apply or the standard leaf. “Payment for” on the left is the original submission text.

listQuickEdit.standardDescription: Pick the normalized standard text for this expense. The original “payment for” text is unchanged.
listQuickEdit.paymentDescription: Choose how this expense was paid.
listQuickEdit.vehicleDescription: Link this expense to a vehicle.
listQuickEdit.saveError: Could not save. Please try again.
listQuickEdit.tapToSetStandard: Tap to set standard payment for
listQuickEdit.openStandardHint: Click to change standard payment for

listInlineEdit.hint: Use the pencil on a row to edit in place. With this on, row clicks will not open the detail dialog.

messages.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this company expense?
messages.suggestionsLoadError: Could not load saved payee or description suggestions. You can still type freely.

adminReceiptOcrParseRules.title: Receipt OCR parse rules
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.description: Controls how OCR text is turned into draft fields (vendor, amount, category, etc.). Stored in shared_settings and used by tour expense OCR and server receipt drafts.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.noPermission: You do not have permission to edit (team position super/admin or allowlisted email).
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionCategoryHint: If OCR text contains a keyword, the paid-for field is prefilled with the label. User rules run first in list order, then built-in defaults.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionPaidToSkipHint: Exclude header-like lines from the merchant-name heuristic. Tested against each trimmed line.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionAmountHintDetail: Amount is searched first on lines matching these patterns. If none match, the default total/heuristic logic runs.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionPreviewHint: Paste sample OCR text to see parsed candidates as JSON with the current rules.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.previewFromDbReceiptsHint: Loads up to 1000 recent rows: those with receipt media, plus «Receipt Pending» (image-only upload) rows. If your login email is on the active team list, the server uses the service role (requires SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY). OCR runs only when image_url or file_path is set.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptsEmpty: No matching tour expenses (scanned latest 1000 rows). Either none exist, or RLS hides them from your JWT session. With SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and an active team row for your email, the full list is returned.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptPickRequired: Select a receipt from the list first.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptOcrLoaded: OCR applied. Change rules below to update the parse preview instantly.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptImageLoadError: Could not load the receipt image. Check URL or storage access.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptOcrNoMediaOnRow: This expense has no image_url/file_path; OCR cannot run.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.receiptNoMediaHint: This row is «Receipt Pending» but media paths are empty. Check the tour expense record or upload/DB pipeline.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepBodyMatch: Body override: “If the full OCR text contains this phrase, force Paid-to / Paid-for” — e.g. horseshoe bend → Horseshoe Bend + Entrance Fee.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepCategory: Category: “If the receipt text contains these words, set Paid-for to …” — e.g. if you see costco, suggest Groceries.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepSkip: Merchant skip: “If a line contains this phrase, don’t treat that line as the store name” — e.g. thank you, www.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideStepAmount: Amount line: “Look for the dollar amount first on lines containing …” — e.g. Total, Grand total. If none match, the built-in logic still runs.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.guideFooter: Use “Body override” for venue-specific receipts. Other rules: add from templates, then Save. Plain phrases are enough; regex is optional under Advanced.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionBodyMatchHint: If the whole OCR text contains the phrase (case-insensitive, spaces normalized), the first matching rule applies. Leave Paid-to, Paid-for, or Payment method blank to keep the automatic guess / card matching.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionBodyMatchPlain: Put more specific phrases higher in the list. The Horseshoe Bend example is under Templates → Body override.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyContains: Phrase contained in full OCR text
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaidToPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep auto guess
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaidForPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep category keywords
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaymentMethodNone: Leave blank to keep card/text matching
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldBodyPaymentCc: If last 4 card digits are found, set payment label to “CC”
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionCategoryPlain: Prefer several short keywords so shaky OCR still matches (mix English/Korean if needed).
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionPaidToSkipPlain: Each rule is tested against a whole line. Open Advanced to edit raw regex if needed.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.sectionAmountPlain: If you add multiple hints, the parser tries those lines first for a dollar amount, then falls back to defaults.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseLabel: Phrase contained in the line (skip as merchant candidate)
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseLabelAmount: Phrase contained in the line (find amount here first)
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseHint: Type normal text only; matching is case-insensitive. It is stored as a safe pattern automatically.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.plainPhraseHintAmount: Short words that usually appear next to the total (Total, Amount due, etc.).
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.legacyPatternHint: This rule was saved as regex only. The plain box may be empty — check Advanced or re-type the phrase.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateDialogIntro: Pick a rule type, then tap a preset. “Body override” pins merchant + paid-for for specific venues.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateKindLabel: What do you want to add?
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateKindSkip: Line to exclude from merchant guess
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateBodyExplain: When the phrase appears anywhere in the OCR text, Paid-to and/or Paid-for are overwritten. Use this when a keyword like “Parking” would win over “Entrance Fee”.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateAddBodyMatch: Add body override with these values
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateCategoryExplain: If the OCR text contains these keywords, the Paid-for field is prefilled with the label.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateSkipExplain: Lines containing this phrase won’t be used as the store-name candidate.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateAmountExplain: The parser looks for a dollar amount on lines that contain this phrase first.
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.templateAddLineRule: Add line rule with this phrase
adminReceiptOcrParseRules.fieldKeywordsExplain: Separate with commas or newlines. Mix EN/KO so OCR typos still match when possible.

adminPaidForLabels.title: Paid-for standard labels
adminPaidForLabels.description: Master list for normalizing the “payment for” text on company expenses. Inactive labels are hidden from pickers but still resolve on linked expenses.
adminPaidForLabels.dialogCreateHint: Leave code empty to auto-generate from the Korean name.
adminPaidForLabels.dialogEditHint: Codes are identifiers; lowercase and underscores are recommended.

expensePaymentMethodNormalize.title: Normalize expense payment methods
expensePaymentMethodNormalize.subtitle: Align stored payment_method strings in reservation, company, and tour expenses with IDs registered in Payment method management. Requires SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY on the server for aggregation and bulk updates.
expensePaymentMethodNormalize.hintStep1: Optionally run “Register legacy values in payment_methods” to create missing rows for raw strings.
expensePaymentMethodNormalize.hintStep2: For “Remap candidate” or “Unmatched” rows, pick the target payment method and click the row action to update the database.
expensePaymentMethodNormalize.hintStep3: Verify labels in Deposits & expense management and Payment method management.
expensePaymentMethodNormalize.migrateNote: Same as POST /api/payment-methods/migrate. Existing IDs are skipped.
expensePaymentMethodNormalize.migrateConfirm: Create payment_methods rows for values that do not exist yet?
expensePaymentMethodNormalize.sameValue: Same as stored value; nothing to do.

passUpload.title: Pass & ID Photo Upload
passUpload.subtitle: Please upload your pass photo and ID photo to register as a non-resident with pass.
passUpload.passPhotoDesc: Please take a photo showing the entire valid pass clearly.
passUpload.idPhotoDesc: Please upload an ID showing your name and photo clearly. (You may cover other personal information)
passUpload.fileFormat: File Format: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP (Max 5MB)
passUpload.privacyNotice: Uploaded information will be deleted after tour completion in accordance with our privacy policy.
passUpload.privacyDesc: Your uploaded pass and ID photos will only be used for tour reservations and operations. They will be immediately deleted after tour completion in accordance with our privacy policy. We collect only the minimum information necessary to provide safe services.
passUpload.idPhotoNotice: Note: Please upload showing only your name and photo. You may cover other personal information (address, SSN, etc.).
passUpload.imageOnly: Only image files are allowed. (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP)
passUpload.saveSuccess: Information saved successfully. Your status will be updated after admin review.
passUpload.bothPhotosRequired: Please upload both pass photo and ID photo.

reservationEvidence.hint: Add images via file upload or paste from clipboard (e.g. US resident / pass proof).
reservationEvidence.addAfterSave: You can add evidence photos after saving the reservation.
